decisions.relationship_evidence: Externally validates and compares eight prehospital early-warning scores for mortality and other adverse outcomes in acute respiratory distress.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit comparative validation study of early-warning-score calibration and discrimination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs a multivariable early-warning prediction model for sepsis-associated encephalopathy using clinical scores and laboratory indicators.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly develops and evaluates a new prediction model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates the prognostic sensitivity and specificity of S100A12, procalcitonin, and MEWS, individually and in combination, in community-acquired pneumonia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a performance evaluation of an early-warning score and biomarkers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares common early-warning scores for predicting in-hospital mortality among emergency-department patients with COVID-19.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study explicitly assesses discrimination and predictive performance of the scores.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses the accuracy, operational impact, and cost-effectiveness of 21 prehospital early-warning scores for identifying sepsis requiring urgent treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a large diagnostic cohort and decision-analytic comparison of early-warning strategies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests whether the National Early Warning Score predicts bacterial rather than viral lower respiratory tract infection and reports that it does not.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record directly evaluates a clinical early-warning score's diagnostic performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates logistic-regression early-warning models, including a SMOTE-enhanced model, for ARDS in patients with sepsis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study explicitly constructs and validates new early-warning models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares MEWS, SAPS II, SOFA, and APACHE II performance and cutoff values for early prediction of septic shock in diabetic patients.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit comparative performance assessment of clinical warning scores.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates early BCG-vaccine complications as clinical warning signs of underlying inborn errors of immunity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study assesses whether observed clinical complications identify an underlying immune disorder.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates 21 early-warning systems for identifying sepsis-related severe maternal outcomes and compares their diagnostic performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit multi-score diagnostic performance study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates the sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values of the Xpert Carba-R assay as an early-warning test for carbapenem-resistant bloodstream infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly validates the assay against sequencing and assesses its warning utility.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews pediatric-sepsis early-warning scores and biomarkers, detailing limitations in accuracy, validation, feasibility, and prognostic use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This methodological review synthesizes limitations and recommends a multifaceted clinical approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews machine-learning early-warning systems for neonatal infection and explicitly identifies validation, false-alarm, user-acceptance, and implementation limitations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record provides explicit guidance about validation and integration requirements for these systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs an early-warning logistic equation for immunocompromised-host sepsis from NLR, CRP, and procalcitonin.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study explicitly develops and assesses a new warning equation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Prospectively compares five prehospital early-warning scores for predicting one- and two-year mortality after suspected respiratory infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record evaluates long-term prognostic performance using ROC analyses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Modifies NEWS2 risk strata for emphysematous pyelonephritis and evaluates the resulting score's prognostic accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly develops a modified warning score and supplies performance estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates AVPU scoring and serum inflammatory markers, separately and jointly, for predicting adverse pediatric respiratory-infection outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit predictive-performance assessment of a score and biomarkers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and externally validates a machine-learning phenotyping method for early identification of high-risk ventilated patients with sepsis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly derives a new early-warning method across multiple datasets.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates MEWS and TREWS for predicting ICU admission, severity, and mortality in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study directly assesses prognostic performance of two warning scores.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates a SMOTE-enhanced early-warning model for postoperative internal-fixation-device infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly constructs a new warning model and compares it with the original-data model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates stratified machine-learning models for early warning of anti-tuberculosis drug-induced liver injury.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly develops, validates, and interprets new warning models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integratively reviews and compares 12 prehospital early-warning scales for COVID-19 deterioration and mortality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The review synthesizes comparative score performance and discusses readiness for clinical use.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates machine-learning early-warning models for carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The study explicitly develops, calibrates, validates, and interprets a new warning model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The scoping review maps symptoms, outcomes, risk factors, and diagnostic approaches for toxoplasmosis but does not use, develop, or evaluate a clinical early-warning score or biomarker method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Its phrase 'early warning signs' describes symptoms rather than a defined score or biomarker methodology for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses NEWS-2 discrimination, calibration, and cutoff performance for mortality in patients aged 80 years or older with infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit prognostic validation of an early-warning score.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a 2×2 factorial cluster-randomized controlled trial across general practices to evaluate removal of incentives and audit feedback.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete chlamydia-testing application of a cluster-randomized design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a two-arm randomized controlled trial clustered by general practice to evaluate an antibiotic-stewardship intervention.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete respiratory-infection application of cluster randomization.
supported_label_variants: randomised controlled trial clustered by general practice

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a large retrospective cohort, exposed-versus-unexposed coverage comparison, covariate adjustment, and IPTW to estimate a primary-care program's impact on AIDS incidence and mortality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete comparative policy-impact cohort evaluation in HIV/AIDS.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Builds a comparative risk-assessment simulation model and generalized framework for prioritizing TB versus MRSA screening.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly develops a reusable screening-prioritization framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses interrupted time series in five intervention wards and 35 control wards to evaluate an infection-control collaborative.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete controlled interrupted-time-series application to C. difficile prevention.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time-series analysis comparing flooded and nonflooded areas to estimate post-flood infectious-health risks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete infectious-disease application with an explicit control series.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a prospective controlled interrupted time series to evaluate audit and feedback on broad-spectrum antimicrobial use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit antimicrobial-stewardship application of the design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a controlled interrupted time-series method with vancomycin as the comparison series to evaluate carbapenem stewardship.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete controlled interrupted-time-series evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series with cardiovascular and coronary-care ICUs as controls to assess antimicrobial-stewardship effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit ICU antimicrobial-stewardship application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses interrupted time series across 17 hospitals implementing chlorhexidine bathing and 16 control hospitals.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The comparison hospitals provide an explicit control series for an infection-prevention evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses segmented interrupted-time-series regression and community-acquired bacteremia as a nonequivalent dependent control to evaluate an infection-control initiative.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a controlled policy-impact application to hospital-onset bacteremia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a controlled interrupted time-series study and controlled segmented regression to assess integrated HIV and hypertension care.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit infectious-health-service application of the design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares an interrupted time series in an intervention department with a nonintervention control group to evaluate antimicrobial stewardship.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit controlled interrupted-time-series application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses comparative interrupted time series to compare longitudinal HIV-visit trends among dual-role and single-role community health workers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HIV-program application with a comparison group.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series and segmented regression with hospitals in another region as comparators to evaluate a CDI initiative.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit regional infection-prevention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a comparative interrupted time-series approach with four control states to evaluate mandated sepsis-care regulations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete sepsis-policy application of comparative interrupted time series.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series with rotavirus-negative cases as the control series to estimate vaccine impact.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit vaccination-impact application of the design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a 14-year controlled interrupted time-series study with community-acquired resistance and antimicrobial use as controls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete antimicrobial-resistance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a comparative interrupted time-series framework with other study sites as the comparison group to estimate flood-related enteric-infection risks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit comparative infectious-disease application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs controlled interrupted time-series analysis with a general-surgery control ward to evaluate antimicrobial stewardship during a KPC outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete outbreak-related controlled ITS application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series with mixed-effects modeling to compare service use in Ebola-affected and control health zones.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit Ebola-policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses comparative interrupted time-series analyses to compare TB notifications in intervention and control local-government areas.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete TB outreach application with control areas.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a controlled interrupted time series comparing hand-hygiene adherence in outbreak and nonoutbreak control units.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit hospital-outbreak application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses interrupted time-series analysis with age-group control series to assess infection hospitalizations after MMR revaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination application with explicit control series.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series and a control series of people not receiving early resistance testing to evaluate an HIV-testing policy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit HIV-policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses retrospective controlled interrupted time series with the white population as the control series to assess Ramadan-associated infection risk.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit infection-risk application of the design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Demonstrates comparative interrupted time series in a multisite evaluation of point-of-care C-reactive-protein testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The paper explicitly provides methodological considerations and guidance for using the design in diagnostic evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses interrupted time series and intervention-versus-control hospital groups to compare changes in cancer operations during COVID-19 waves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The analysis explicitly estimates between-group changes around interruption points.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a controlled interrupted time-series approach to quantify changes in rural hospital use following the COVID-19 outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit pandemic-healthcare application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series with intervention and control sexual-health services to evaluate rapid STI testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit STI-service application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a two-stage controlled interrupted time-series design to estimate effects of COVID-19 interventions on ten infectious diseases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit multi-disease policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a controlled interrupted time-series design with matched facilities to evaluate hepatitis-B birth-dose monitoring.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit vaccination-program application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series to compare vaccination rates in mandate-targeted and control age groups in Greece and Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record contains concrete controlled analyses of COVID-19 vaccine mandates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series with the rest of Australia as a control series to assess cancer-therapy changes during COVID-19 waves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit pandemic-impact application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a multinational controlled interrupted time-series study with intervention and control physicians to evaluate automated prescribing feedback.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit antimicrobial-stewardship application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses register-based controlled interrupted time series with matched control parishes to estimate local-lockdown effects on SARS-CoV-2 incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit COVID-19 policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time series to estimate effects of outreach clinics on COVID-19 vaccine uptake.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-outreach application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses interrupted time-series predictions and an ineligible age-group comparison to estimate effects of universal RSV immunization.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a vaccination-impact ITS application with a comparison group.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses inverse-probability weighting and a balanced interrupted time series with control to compare treated and counterfactual HCV populations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit HCV-treatment application of a controlled ITS design.
supported_label_variants: balanced interrupted time series with control

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses controlled interrupted time-series analyses with same-age boys as controls to assess infection hospitalizations after HPV-vaccine introduction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit multicountry vaccination application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an age-structured deterministic model to compare counterfactual pertussis booster strategies and their effects on infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-strategy impact model.
supported_label_variants: modelling the impact of extended vaccination strategies

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits epidemic models to estimate meningitis cases averted under alternative vaccination timing and coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-response impact model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The mathematical model estimates how many vaccine recipients had already been infected before vaccination, rather than estimating a policy or vaccination program's counterfactual impact.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not compare vaccination-impact or policy counterfactuals for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Models polio burden under the explicit counterfactual absence of India's elimination program and attributes factual-counterfactual differences to the program.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete counterfactual vaccination-policy impact evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Markov-chain Monte Carlo simulation model to project cases prevented by a proposed hepatitis-B immunization program.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete prospective vaccination-impact model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses dynamic transmission modeling to compare the health and economic impact of six dengue-vaccination programs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-strategy impact and cost-effectiveness model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a decision-tree and Markov model to compare maternal GBS immunization with no immunization and project cases, deaths, and DALYs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete counterfactual vaccination-impact model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a population model to estimate cases, hospitalizations, deaths, and costs under maternal pertussis vaccination added to the existing program.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-policy impact model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a decision-tree model to estimate the theoretical counterfactual impact and economic value of routine yellow-fever vaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-impact evaluation against a no-program scenario.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses two model variants and multiple vaccination scenarios to estimate yellow-fever burden, vaccine demand, infections averted, and deaths averted.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccine-impact modeling application informing the EYE strategy.
supported_label_variants: Vaccine demand forecast and impact modelling

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an SEIR model under alternative transmission scenarios to estimate how underreporting changes the apparent impact of vaccination strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record concretely models vaccination-strategy impact, although its novel method chiefly concerns underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses vaccine- and age-structured models to compare vaccination and prioritization scenarios for COVID-19 in West Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete counterfactual vaccination-strategy analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a COVID-19 modeling framework for simulating vaccine rollout, efficacy, waning immunity, variants, behavior, and government-policy scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record explicitly develops a reusable policy and vaccination impact model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs explicit no-vaccine counterfactual case counts to estimate cases averted by Sweden's routine vaccination programs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete counterfactual vaccination-impact evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Permutes estimated reproduction numbers across countries to estimate mortality under counterfactual adoption of other countries' COVID-19 policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete comparative counterfactual policy-impact assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates counterfactual deaths without COVID-19 vaccination and derives deaths averted across 17 Latin American and Caribbean countries.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete population-level counterfactual vaccine-impact estimate.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an age-structured transmission model to compare alternative vaccination-prioritization scenarios, including a no-vaccination scenario.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-strategy impact model for the Republic of Korea.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian SEIR model to compare Sweden's factual vaccination trajectory with an explicit unvaccinated counterfactual.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete counterfactual vaccination-impact analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends robust-mixture-prior dynamic borrowing of historical controls to adjust for covariates using IPTW, G-computation, and doubly robust estimation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is an explicit methodological development with simulation-based performance evaluation.
supported_label_variants: Dynamic borrowing of historical controls adjusting for covariates

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an event-study design and difference-in-difference-in-differences models to estimate effects of parental-involvement laws on STI outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete STI-policy application of legacy event-study and DDD methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports difference-in-differences estimates comparing pre/post antibiotic-prescribing changes across randomized intervention and control practices.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete antibiotic-stewardship application of difference-in-differences estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The abstract describes staggered PulseNet adoption as a natural experiment but does not explicitly state that a difference-in-differences or event-study method was used.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Staggered adoption alone is insufficient to establish a relationship to the exact canonical design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a difference-in-differences design with coarsened exact matching to estimate effects of displacement on child health in Haiti.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete post-disaster infectious-health application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a quasi-experimental difference-in-differences approach to estimate the effect of gifts on consent to home-based HIV testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HIV-testing application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference-in-differences with a propensity-score-matched control group to estimate refugee effects on maternal and child health services.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This includes concrete vaccination and health-service outcomes in a refugee-hosting setting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference-in-differences as a sensitivity analysis of associations between procalcitonin use and antibiotic or clinical outcomes in sepsis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The design is explicitly applied to a concrete sepsis-management question.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a retrospective difference-in-differences design to estimate effects of antibiotic-prophylaxis timing on surgical-site infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete infection-prevention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference-in-differences with nonpneumonia hospitalization as the control condition to estimate pneumococcal-vaccine impact.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete vaccination-impact application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference in differences with preimplementation eligible patients and generalized estimating equations to evaluate differentiated ART delivery.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HIV-service application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts difference-in-differences analysis exploiting staggered voluntary licensing to estimate effects on hepatitis-C treatment uptake.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HCV policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a difference-in-differences approach to a natural experiment removing fibrosis restrictions on hepatitis-C treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HCV access-policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference in differences to compare childhood diarrhoea changes in countries that extended paid maternity leave with countries that did not.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete infectious-health policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates differences-in-differences, with IPTW adjustment, to evaluate an HIV stigma-reduction intervention in Ghanaian health facilities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete HIV-intervention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an event-study framework across US states to estimate immediate and subsequent information-seeking responses to first COVID-19 case announcements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete COVID-19 event-study application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses difference-in-differences analysis to estimate effects of Japan's primary-care registration policy on healthcare and antibiotic use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete antibiotic-policy application.
